title: Listing Addressees — Both 'To:' and 'Distribution:' Lines
source_text: 8-2c. Using Both a "To:" Line and "Distribution:" Line. Use both the "To:" line and the "Distribution:" line in the same letter when you show a group title whose distribution is relatively unknown. Place the group title ("Area Records Officers," for example) in the "To:" line and identify each member in a "Distribution:" line. See figure 8-3.
plain_language_rule: When a multiple-address letter is addressed to a group or collective whose individual membership or distribution is relatively unknown — such as a functional group title — use both a 'To:' line and a 'Distribution:' line. Place the group title in the 'To:' line and list the individual members or addressees in the 'Distribution:' line.
renderer_impact: This rule selects the address-line format (both To: and Distribution: lines) when a group title is the To: addressee. The renderer must detect group-title patterns to trigger this format. Specific line spacing, placement, and formatting are determined by figure 8-3 and are rendering concerns not encoded in this rule.
notes: Source text resolved 2026-05-11 from manual overview text. The manual does not mention 'board' or 'committee' in 8-2c; it uses 'group title' with examples 'Area Records Officers' (overview) and 'Standard of Conduct Coordinators' (figure 8-3). Whether Distribution addressees in this format are classified as action addressees is not explicitly stated in 8-2c (contrast 8-2b which does specify). Copy-to relationship is not addressed within 8-2c; general Chapter 7 Copy to rules apply. This is a distinct format from C8-002 (To: line only, individual action addressees) and C8-003 (Distribution: line only, >4 or variable copies).
